summary: Design a pixel-accurate low-poly wireframe face-profile hero illustration to match a reference; judge approaches; synthesize a definitive implementation recipe
logs: Consensus winner: Approach A — Hand-structured 2D inline-SVG low-poly face (Delaunay + concavity clip) — Approach A — Hand-structured 2D inline-SVG low-poly face (Delaunay + concavity clip):169, Approach D — Hybrid crisp-SVG face + Canvas particle field + CSS glow:87, Approach D — Hybrid crisp-SVG face mesh + CSS/RAF particle field:85, Approach D — Hybrid crisp-SVG face + CSS/RAF ambient particle field:85, Approach A — Hand-structured inline-SVG low-poly face (Delaunay + concavity clip):84, Approach B — 2D Canvas, Bowyer–Watson Delaunay + variable-radius Poisson-disk:83, Approach B — 2D Canvas, Poisson-disk interior + Bowyer-Watson, additive glow:83, Approach B — 2D Canvas, Bowyer-Watson + variable-radius Poisson interior:79, Approach C — Orthographic R3F neural profile (no extrude, no rotation):74, Approach C — Orthographic R3F 'Neural Profile' (Delaunay point-cloud):74, Approach C — Orthographic R3F 'Neural Profile' (no extrude, no rotation):70

result.winner: Approach A — Hand-structured 2D inline-SVG low-poly face (Delaunay + concavity clip)

blueprint.chosenRenderer: 2D inline SVG (single React client component emitting one `<svg>` with `<line>`/`<path>`/`<circle>` elements), wrapped in a `position:relative; overflow:hidden` div whose CSS `background` supplies the radial violet glow. NO WebGL/R3F (the prior blob failure was 3D; the reference is flat; headless Chrome runs `--disable-gpu` in this project's PDF pipeline so WebGL is blank-prone). NO `feGaussianBlur` anywhere — every "glow" is done filter-free (CSS radial-gradient for the background bloom, a `radialGradient`-filled `<circle>` for node blooms, and a wide low-opacity stroked path under a crisp core stroke for the contour halo), because SVG blur is the flakiest primitive in headless rasterizers. NO Canvas overlay — particles are SVG circles inside one `<g>` that the whole group drifts via a single cheap CSS transform, so connector lines stay valid and the layer is deterministic. All geometry is computed ONCE in `useMemo` with a seeded mulberry32 PRNG (SSR-safe, byte-identical every render/screenshot). Animation is CSS-only (per-node twinkle via seeded animation-delay, group drift), so a t=0 headless capture is already the finished composition and `prefers-reduced-motion` degrades to a static frame with a one-line media query. Backbone = Approach A (inline SVG + Delaunay + concavity clip + place() left-remap + A's complete layer spec); grafts = Approach C's higher-resolution nose/lip/chin coordinates (judged the most refined face), Approach B's focus-seeded variable-radius Bridson Poisson-disk interior + particle connector lines, and Approach D's CSS-gradient glow + CSS-only twinkle + filter-free contour halo.
blueprint.faceProfilePoints:
// LEFT-FACING profile, normalized [x,y] in its OWN [0,1] space (x increases RIGHT, y increases DOWN).
// place() later compresses this into the LEFT ~62% of the container so the right third stays empty for particles.
// Invariants that MUST hold (they are what make it read as a face, not a blob):
//   - NOSE TIP (idx 12) is the global x-MINIMUM (0.155) -> the nose unmistakably juts left.
//   - nasion(0.294) -> bridge(0.300) -> ridge(0.235) -> approaching-tip(0.190) -> TIP(0.155) is a clean nose slope.
//   - lip run out/in/out: upper-lip vermilion(0.214) , mouth notch(0.236) , lower-lip(0.223).
//   - CHIN (idx 21, 0.256) sits LEFT of the under-lip sulcus(0.246? see note) and well left of under-chin(0.300) so it protrudes.
//   - BACK OF HEAD (idx 34, 0.830) is the global x-MAXIMUM.
//   - shoulders (idx 27,28) are pushed to y>=1.01 so the bust exits the bottom cleanly under overflow:hidden.
const PROFILE = [
  [0.500,0.060], // 0  crown top
  [0.420,0.074], // 1  front crown
  [0.360,0.110], // 2  upper forehead (rounded)
  [0.325,0.170], // 3  forehead
  [0.305,0.230], // 4  lower forehead
  [0.300,0.280], // 5  brow ridge (slight bump)
  [0.316,0.312], // 6  brow -> eye
  [0.294,0.335], // 7  nasion / eye-socket dip (most recessed of upper face)
  [0.300,0.365], // 8  top of nose bridge
  [0.275,0.400], // 9  nose bridge
  [0.235,0.435], // 10 nose ridge
  [0.190,0.468], // 11 nose approaching tip
  [0.155,0.495], // 12 NOSE TIP  <- global leftmost
  [0.198,0.506], // 13 columella (under nose)
  [0.246,0.516], // 14 subnasale / philtrum base
  [0.234,0.536], // 15 upper-lip protrusion
  [0.214,0.551], // 16 upper-lip vermilion (out)
  [0.236,0.566], // 17 mouth notch (in)
  [0.223,0.586], // 18 lower lip (out)
  [0.246,0.610], // 19 mentolabial sulcus (in, under-lip dip)
  [0.234,0.635], // 20 chin front
  [0.256,0.662], // 21 CHIN TIP (protrudes)
  [0.300,0.696], // 22 under-chin
  [0.372,0.722], // 23 jaw underside
  [0.420,0.752], // 24 front neck (top)
  [0.440,0.822], // 25 front neck
  [0.454,0.900], // 26 neck -> chest front
  [0.470,1.010], // 27 front shoulder base (exits bottom)
  [0.760,1.010], // 28 back shoulder base (exits bottom)
  [0.702,0.862], // 29 lower back neck
  [0.686,0.782], // 30 nape
  [0.702,0.700], // 31 skull base
  [0.752,0.620], // 32 occiput lower
  [0.800,0.520], // 33 back of head rising
  [0.830,0.420], // 34 BACK OF HEAD  <- global rightmost (big rounded curve)
  [0.824,0.320], // 35 upper back skull
  [0.780,0.220], // 36 back skull
  [0.710,0.130], // 37 back crown
  [0.620,0.078], // 38 crown back (closes to idx 0)
]; // 39 pts. Density attractors (same normalized space): eye[0.325,0.300], nose[0.205,0.460], lips[0.240,0.550], chin[0.255,0.640].
blueprint.meshAlgorithm:
DETERMINISTIC, built ONCE in useMemo. mulberry32(seed=20260711). All coords are mapped to PX first via place(), then everything is computed in px.

0) COMPOSITION REMAP (place): HX0=0.06, HX1=0.72, HY0=0.02, HY1=1.00.
   place(nx,ny) = [ (HX0+(HX1-HX0)*nx)*W , (HY0+(HY1-HY0)*ny)*H ].
   -> head bbox lands roughly x in [0.15W .. 0.61W]; the right ~34% (>=0.72W) is guaranteed empty for particles. Enforce as an invariant: if you edit PROFILE, keep max placed x <= 0.66*W and start the particle band at >= 0.78*W. This is the single composition rule the reference demands.

1) DENSITY FIELD (drives BOTH interior spacing and contour resampling):
   feats = FEATURES.map(place). minFeat(x,y) = min distance to any placed feature attractor.
   radiusAt(x,y) = R_MIN + (R_MAX-R_MIN) * clamp( minFeat(x,y) / (0.42*W), 0, 1 ),  R_MIN=13px, R_MAX=34px.
   => ~13px spacing at eye/nose/lips (dense), growing to ~34px at the back of the skull (sparse). Exactly the reference's information gradient.

2) CONTOUR VERTICES (seed the outline INTO the point set so every triangle edge terminates exactly on the profile -> no ragged rim):
   For each PROFILE segment a->b: step = radiusAt(midpoint)*0.72; n = max(1, round(len/step)); push n evenly spaced points. Mark indices < cN as onContour=true. ~90-120 boundary nodes.

3) INTERIOR via VARIABLE-RADIUS BRIDSON POISSON-DISK (active-list), seeded at the eye/cheek focus place(0.32,0.40):
   - Background grid, cell = R_MIN/sqrt(2). k = 14 candidate darts per active point.
   - candidate angle uniform, distance in [r, 2r] with r = radiusAt(activePoint).
   - fits(x,y) is TRUE only if: inPoly(x,y) AND distToPoly(x,y) >= 0.55*R_MIN (kills rim doubling that smears) AND no existing neighbor within min(radiusAt(cand), radiusAt(neighbor)) (search grid span = ceil(R_MAX/cell)+1).
   - This guarantees a minimum gap (no clumping -> the direct fix for the prior white smear) and blooms density outward from the face. ~140-220 interior nodes.

4) TRIANGULATE all nodes (contour + interior) with inline Bowyer-Watson Delaunay (super-triangle 10x bbox, removed at end). ~250-340 pts total, runs <5ms once. No external dep. (If you ever push >600 pts, swap in Delaunator/d3-delaunay as a drop-in.)

5) CONCAVITY CLIP (turns the convex hull into a readable profile - the whole game):
   Keep a triangle ONLY if (a) its CENTROID passes inPoly (deletes triangles that web across the empty wedge in front of the nose, the mouth gap, and under the chin) AND (b) its longest edge < MAX_EDGE = 58px (drops long spanners across the sparse back). Verify the mouth gap and nose wedge stay OPEN after any profile edit.

6) UNIQUE EDGES: dedupe into a Set('min_max'). Per edge, at its midpoint m:
   cp = clamp(1 - distToPoly(m)/38, 0, 1)   // contour proximity
   fp = clamp(1 - minFeat(m)/(0.20*W), 0, 1) // feature proximity
   opacity o = clamp(0.15 + 0.50*cp + 0.22*fp, 0.13, 0.85);  hot = fp > 0.35.
   => rim + feature edges brightest; deep interior faint.

7) NODE BRIGHTNESS (the "brighter toward the face" rule):
   cn = onContour ? 1 : clamp(1 - distToPoly(x,y)/34, 0, 1);
   ft = clamp(1 - minFeat(x,y)/(0.22*W), 0, 1);
   b  = clamp(0.24 + 0.50*cn + 0.34*ft, 0, 1).
   => brightest nodes RING the profile and CLUSTER at eye/nose/lips; dim deep inside.

8) BLOOM SUBSET: take nodes with b > 0.78, sort b desc, keep the top 8. Only these get a soft radialGradient bloom. (Cap the count - many blooms re-create the smear.)

9) PARTICLES (mesh dissolving into empty right space): 30 dots, x in [0.78,0.99]*W, y in [0.12,0.86]*H, r 0.6-1.7, o 0.15-0.55. Precompute connector LINES between any pair < 72px apart (opacity 0.10-0.18 by 1-d/72). Because the whole particle <g> drifts by ONE CSS transform, the precomputed links remain correct.

Helpers: inPoly = even-odd ray cast; distToPoly = min point-to-segment over all edges; delaunay = Bowyer-Watson with inline circumcircle test.
blueprint.renderRecipe:
Back-to-front. Exact values. "Brighter toward the face" is enforced by node brightness b (step 7), edge opacity (step 6), and the vertical contour gradient (front/top hottest).

L0 BACKGROUND + GLOW — on the WRAPPER div (not the SVG; CSS gradient is the most headless/print-reliable glow):
  background:
    radial-gradient(58% 55% at 33% 40%, rgba(124,58,237,0.35), rgba(124,58,237,0) 70%),
    radial-gradient(30% 30% at 30% 38%, rgba(168,85,247,0.20), transparent 62%),
    #030408;
  The bloom sits behind the cheek/eye. The SVG on top is transparent.

L1 EDGES (crisp wireframe, single stroke each, NO glow filter):
  cool edges: stroke #5E3CE8, width 0.7, per-edge opacity o (step 6).
  hot edges (fp>0.35): stroke #8B6EF2, width 1.0, per-edge opacity o.
  (Neon feel comes from the L0 glow bleeding through crossings + the contour, not from blurred edges.)

L2 PARTICLES <g class="fm-drift"> (drawn before the face so they read as behind/around it on the right):
  connector lines: stroke #5E3CE8, width 0.5, opacity 0.10-0.18.
  dots: fill #8B6EF2, r 0.6-1.7, their own opacities 0.15-0.55.

L3 CONTOUR HALO (filter-free): the closed profile path,
  stroke #A855F7, width 5, opacity 0.16, fill none, strokeLinejoin round.
L3b optional mid-halo: same path, stroke #8B6EF2, width 2.6, opacity 0.22 (adds body without blur).

L4 CONTOUR CORE (the single brightest, thickest, unbroken line — the definitive "it's a face" cue):
  same path, stroke url(#contourGrad), width 1.7, opacity 0.98, strokeLinejoin round.
  contourGrad = linearGradient top->bottom: 0% #8B6EF2 -> 55% #A855F7 -> 100% #C4B5FD (front/top of face hottest).

L5 NODE BLOOMS (top-8 only): <circle r=7 fill="url(#nodeBloom)"> centered on each bloom node.
  nodeBloom = radialGradient: 0% stopColor #C4B5FD stopOpacity 0.55 -> 65% #C4B5FD stopOpacity 0 -> 100% transparent.

L6 NODES CRISP (radii capped <=2.2px so they NEVER merge into a mass):
  r = 0.8 + b*1.4.
  fill = b>0.82 ? #E9D5FF : b>0.60 ? #C4B5FD : b>0.40 ? #8B6EF2 : #5E3CE8.
  opacity = 0.40 + 0.60*b.
  Nodes with b>0.55 get class "fm-tw" and inline style { ['--o']: opacity, opacity: 'var(--o)' } plus seeded animationDelay/Duration.

L7 (DEBUG, off by default) when debug=true: render each placed PROFILE point index as tiny #E9D5FF text — the drag-tuning overlay for nailing nose/lips/chin against the reference.

defs needed: linearGradient#contourGrad, radialGradient#nodeBloom. NO filters.
blueprint.motionAndA11y:
MOTION (CSS-only, no RAF, no JS animation loop):
 1) Node twinkle — only nodes with b>0.55 carry class "fm-tw"; @keyframes fmTw { 0%,100%{opacity:var(--o)} 50%{opacity:1} }. Each gets a seeded animation-delay (0-4s) and duration (2.6-4.4s) so they shimmer out of phase. Base opacity var(--o) is the node's static opacity, peak 1 -> a gentle brighten, never a flash.
 2) Particle drift — the whole particle <g class="fm-drift"> gets @keyframes fmDrift { from{transform:translate(0,0)} to{transform:translate(14px,-10px)} } over 22s ease-in-out infinite alternate. Because it is one transform on the group, the precomputed connector lines stay attached. Very slow, no spin, no head movement (the anti-blob rule: the face geometry NEVER translates/rotates).

REDUCED MOTION: the keyframes live inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference){ .fm-anim .fm-tw{...} .fm-anim .fm-drift{...} }. So under reduce (or when the animate prop is false, which removes the fm-anim class), NOTHING animates and every node/particle renders at its base state.

SCREENSHOT / HEADLESS: the very first painted frame IS the finished image — twinkle keyframe 0% == base opacity, drift translate(0,0). No "animate-in". Geometry is synchronous in useMemo, seeded, SSR-safe -> byte-identical every capture. For CI/PDF pass animate={false} (or run Chrome with --force-prefers-reduced-motion) to guarantee the deterministic baseline. Set overflow:hidden on the wrapper so the y>=1.01 shoulder points clip cleanly into a bust.

A11Y: svg role="img" aria-label="Neural-network portrait, a left-facing human face profile rendered as a glowing low-poly mesh". Decorative particles inherit the single label. No motion for users who opt out; no flashing (twinkle is a slow opacity ramp, well under 3Hz).
blueprint.tuningNotes:
CONVERGING ON THE REFERENCE (do this with debug overlay on, animate off):
- The profile is the whole illusion. Render with debug={true} to label the 39 PROFILE points, screenshot at 520x480, and nudge ONLY the front-face run (idx 8-22). Keep the four invariants: idx12 stays the global x-min (nose tip), idx34 the x-max (back of head), lip run out(16)/in(17)/out(18), chin(21) left of under-chin(22).
- Nose strength is the one contested call. The judges split: one wanted A's aggressive 0.088 tip, another wanted a gentle 0.21-0.27. This ships C's 0.155 as the balanced middle (clearly a jutting nose, not a witch-hook, not a flat face). If it reads too soft at final size, drag idx12 to ~0.12; if too caricatured, to ~0.18. Move idx11 and idx13 with it so the slope and columella stay smooth.
- If the mouth looks noisy/oscillating, reduce the amplitude of idx15-19 (pull them toward a common x ~0.230) — legibility beats anatomical detail at 520px.
- Composition: head should fill left ~62% with a genuinely empty right third. If the head sits too small/left, raise HX1 (0.72 -> 0.76). If particles overlap the skull, push the particle band start up (0.78 -> 0.82) and re-check max placed x <= 0.66*W.
- Density: if the interior looks too skeletal, lower R_MIN (13 -> 11) or R_MAX (34 -> 30) for more nodes; if it smears/crowds, raise them. Feature density is controlled by the 0.42*W denominator in radiusAt (smaller = tighter dense zone).
- Concavity check after ANY profile edit: confirm the wedge in front of the nose, the mouth gap, and the under-chin notch stay EMPTY. If a triangle bridges a concavity, lower MAX_EDGE (58 -> 50). A rare sliver surviving the centroid test near the deep lip-notch is the classic offender.
- Glow balance: the "brighter toward the face" read comes from (a) the CSS background bloom centered at 33%/40% (move it to sit behind the eye/cheek) and (b) node brightness b. If the back of the head glows too much, raise the ft denominator (0.22*W) or lower the cn contribution.

COMMON PITFALLS (all pre-solved here, keep them solved):
- White smear: never raise node radius above ~2.2px, never add blur to ordinary nodes/edges, keep blooms to <=8. Do NOT use additive blending.
- Blank headless capture: this build has no WebGL and no blur filters, the two things that go wrong under --disable-gpu. Keep it that way.
- Random twinkle phase in screenshots: pass animate={false} for CI/PDF, or run Chrome with --force-prefers-reduced-motion, so you capture the deterministic base frame.
- Hydration mismatch: all randomness is mulberry32(constant). Never introduce Math.random or Date-based seeds.
- Flat-cut bust: the wrapper MUST keep overflow:hidden so the y>=1.01 shoulder points clip into a bust rather than showing a hard line.
- Bowyer-Watson is O(n^2) and slightly fragile on cocircular points; the seeded Poisson jitter avoids degeneracies at the current ~250-340 pts. If you push past ~600 pts, memoize harder or drop in Delaunator/d3-delaunay (it is a straight swap for delaunay()).
- Performance: hundreds of DOM nodes twinkling via CSS is compositor-cheap; if a very low-end target janks, gate the fm-tw class to only b>0.7 nodes.
